routes.corrections: Given data listed pitches=0 and no length/grade; Mountain Project confirms YDS 5.6, ~90 ft (27 m), a single pitch. FA remains genuinely unknown/uncredited per MP (matches the given "unknown" value) — old hardware found in place suggests an unrecorded early ascent.

routes.fa: Christian Junkar, Tom Beirne, Mik Metzler, August 2020
routes.rappels: Descend via the wall-top fixed rap tree (60 m rope reported as of Sep 2019 — inspect before trusting, reported pika damage to gear left at anchors) or the scree walk-off using full rope length as a handline/backup.
objHaz: Variable/loose rock typical of a young alpine line
routes.face: Right side of Osprey Wall, on white marbled rock
routes.overview: Prey climbs a 5-pitch (510 ft), mostly-bolted line up corners, roofs, and an arete on the right side of Osprey Wall on quality white marbled rock, culminating in a celebrated 5.10c "money pitch."
routes.beta: A series of corners, overhangs, and slabs to the top of Osprey Wall with excellent crystal pocket holds; protection is a mix of bolts and natural gear, with the third pitch fully bolted. Pitch 1 ("First Flight," 5.6) is a bolted slab ending in a chute; P2 (5.9+) climbs a right-facing corner with a roof and bolted slab; P3 (5.10c, "the money pitch") features roof systems and an arete on white marbled rock; P4 (5.9) follows a corner ramp becoming an open book (with two variations); P5 (5.10c) finishes on an arete.
routes.approach: From the west-side pullout across from the Alpental neighborhood entrance off I-90 Exit 52 (Alpental Road), a maintained trail through the woods (~5 min) reaches a large boulder and scree field; ascend up and left past the end of the rockfall to the mossy ramp (~15 min total from the car), then continue following cairns and trees flagged with climbing rope to the base of P1.
routes.descent: Rappel/scree descent from the top of the wall.
routes.descentText: From the top of the wall, a fixed rap tree (60 m rope reported as of Sep 2019) allows a single-rope rappel — inspect any fixed gear before trusting it due to reported pika damage at anchors. Alternatively, descend the scree slope, using the full rope length as a handline/backup if downclimbing.
routes.turnaround: As a short, low-elevation multi-pitch crag route with a fast approach, turnaround decisions should be based on remaining daylight, party pace on the harder pitches, and incoming weather rather than a fixed clock time.
routes.bestSeason: Best in summer (roughly June-September) once the mossy lower rock has fully dried; the approach can hold steep snow into early season and rock quality is notably worse when wet.
routes.detailedRack: Double ropes; ~13 quickdraws; offset cams; Totems; small nuts; long slings for the wandering corner and roof systems.
routes.proNeeds: Mixed bolted and gear-protected climbing (P3 is fully bolted); bring offset cams, Totems, and small nuts for the corner/roof pitches, plus long slings to manage rope drag, and double ropes for the descent.
whatToBring: Helmet (loose rock typical of a young alpine route)
Approach shoes for the scree/boulder approach
Extra sling material for anchors — avoid leaving gear at raps due to reported pika damage
proTips: Pitch 3, "the money pitch," is the highlight — a bolted roof/arete system on quality white marbled rock
Bring offset cams and Totems for the corner and roof systems on P2/P4
Double ropes are recommended both for the pitch 2 roof and for the descent
watchOut: PG13 rating — some runout between bolts on slab sections
Route established 2020 — expect some looser, less-weathered rock in spots
Approach and lower pitches can be mossy/wet if not fully dry
knownHazards: Variable rock quality typical of a young (2020) alpine line
Possible loose rock/moss in shaded or damp sections
Exposed arete finishes on P3 and P5

pitchDetail.notes: Bolted slab ending in a chute; 4 bolts, 30 m.
Right-facing corner with a roof and bolted slab; 5 bolts + gear, 40 m.
Roof systems and an arete on white marbled rock; 11 bolts, 30-40 m.
Corner ramp becoming an open book, with two variations; 3 bolts + gear, 30 m.
Arete finish; 3 bolts, 15 m.
